The '''Phase III Dark Trooper''' was the last and most powerful [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]] exoskeleton/[[Droid/Legends|droid]]. Although in equal height to the [[Phase II Dark Trooper/Legends|Phase II dark trooper]], it was far stronger and more durable. This culminated into the fact it was intended to be the armor of the next generation of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. It was sometimes even classified as a bipedal tank.

==Characteristics==
[[File:DarkTrooper3_egtd.png|thumb|left|120px|Phase III [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]
Had the project come to full realization, it would have produced the perfect battlefield infantry unit—an amalgam of a [[Cloning/Legends|clone's]] creativity, unpredictability, and initiative combined with the resilience and firepower of a heavy [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]]. The exoskeleton provided its wearer with increased firepower, strength, and durability.

The Phase III trooper's standard weapon complement included a large handheld [[Assault cannon/Legends|assault cannon]] and two shoulder-mounted seeker [[Missile/Legends|missile]] launcher racks that were hidden under the suit's shoulder plates when not in use. They were issued additional [[Fragmentation grenade/Legends|fragmentation grenades]], [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], and [[Concussion grenade/Legends|concussion grenades]]. Phase III Dark troopers wore black armor made of the nearly indestructible metal [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]], mined on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Gromas 16]]. It provided superior protection against enemy fire including energy weapons such as [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]].

Some of the Phase III Dark Troopers also were equipped with an array of integrated weaponry, including a pair of dual [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]], two in each wrist, and six [[PLX-1/Legends|PLEX]] rocket tubes located under the shoulder plates, three per shoulder.

==History==
Only a small number of Phase III dark troopers were ever constructed; one was the armor of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Rom Mohc]], who used it as an exosuit in single combat against [[Kyle Katarn]] during a battle onboard the colossal factory ship ''[[Arc Hammer]]''.<ref name="Dark Forces Official Player's Guide">''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]''</ref> However, Mohc was [[Mission to the Arc Hammer|defeated]] and ''Arc Hammer'' was destroyed, costing the Empire billions of credits and thus bringing the Dark Trooper Project to a premature end.

Following the destruction of the ''Arc Hammer'', some units were still left in operation. For instance, some working models of the Phase III dark trooper were present during [[Tyber Zann]]'s [[Raid on the Imperial Archive|theft]] of a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith holocron/Legends|artifact]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'', these droids can only be created on a planet if ''Arc Hammer'' is placed above that specific planet. They were also featured in some of the story missions.

Like [[Imperial Navy Trooper/Legends|several]] [[Flowing Water|other]] people [[Rahm Kota/Legends|and]] [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|things]] in the Star Wars universe, the Phase III dark trooper's appearance evokes the Samurai motif, an acknowledged [[Star Wars#Influences|inspiration]] of Star Wars, by bearing a strong resemblance to [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samurai Samurai armor].

The Phase III Dark Trooper was made into a 3.75 scale action figure in the 1998 "Power Of the Force" line, which was reissued in the 2007 "30th Anniversary Collection" line. The Phase III Dark Troopers were also made into "Build-A-Droid" figures by Hasbro in 2009, which came in pieces in several separate figure 2-packs. Curiously, they appear very distinct from one another; the 1998 figure is accurate to the sprites seen in Dark Forces, while the buildable Dark Trooper seems to have been based off of the render found in "The NEW essential guide to droids" (which is the image seen above).
